title: EEG frequencies and typical waveforms
learning_goals: Identify canonical EEG bands and relate waveform shape to frequency class. | Use waveform morphology as the base layer for downstream qEEG interpretation. | Anchor delta, theta, alpha, beta, and mu terminology to actual trace appearance.
analyzer_use: Use for band-level interpretation guardrails before describing spectral findings in clinical language.
raw_workbench_use: Use when counting oscillations manually, distinguishing band classes, and aligning raw waveform shape with qEEG outputs.

title: Psychopharmacology part 1: clinical presentation confounds
learning_goals: Account for prescribed and non-prescribed substances before interpreting EEG-driven clinical presentation. | Identify caffeine and nicotine as practical qEEG confounds that alter arousal and training readiness.
analyzer_use: Use before interpreting arousal or symptom-linked qEEG findings when caffeine, nicotine, or medication exposure may be present.
raw_workbench_use: Use as a prescan checklist so activated or dysregulated raw EEG is not misread without substance context.

title: Psychopharmacology part 2: EEG measure confounds
learning_goals: Map stimulants and illicit substances to likely alpha, theta, and beta changes on EEG. | Treat drug-related band shifts as confounds before assigning pathology labels.
analyzer_use: Use when a profile shows beta excess, alpha suppression, or reduced theta that may be medication-driven.
raw_workbench_use: Use when generalized fast activity or altered posterior alpha may reflect stimulant exposure rather than intrinsic dysfunction.

title: Identifying and reducing artifacts
learning_goals: Recognize common recording artifacts before editing or reporting qEEG abnormalities. | Reduce false positives during manual raw review.
analyzer_use: Use as a guardrail before treating outlier power or asymmetry as brain-based.
raw_workbench_use: Use directly during channel-by-channel artifact review and segment rejection.

title: Topographic maps and qEEG report writing
learning_goals: Interpret qEEG head maps without separating them from raw EEG and clinical context. | Translate findings into report language appropriate for clinicians and treatment planning.
analyzer_use: Use when summarizing map-based deviations into clinician-facing interpretations.
raw_workbench_use: Use after raw validation so report statements stay grounded in reviewed signal quality.

title: Ethics, professional conduct, and efficacy criteria
learning_goals: Apply ethical and professional limits to qEEG communication and treatment claims. | Separate evidence-supported use cases from overreach.
analyzer_use: Use when framing uncertainty, limitations, and strength of inference in AI-facing qEEG narratives.
raw_workbench_use: Use as a reporting boundary so technical observations are not overstated clinically.

title: Abnormal EEG patterns
learning_goals: Recognize non-benign EEG patterns that require escalation beyond routine qEEG coaching. | Distinguish signal quality issues from clinically meaningful abnormal morphology.
analyzer_use: Use as a caution layer before benignly summarizing patterns that may warrant formal EEG review.
raw_workbench_use: Use during manual review when spikes, slowing, or unusual morphology need escalation.
